perturbation.original_text: Customer agrees to pay the fees and other charges for the Hosting Services and other services provided under this Agreement as specified in Exhibit A of this Agreement. CUSTOMER AGREES TO PAY FOR HOSTING SERVICES ON OR BEFORE THE FIRST DAY OF THE MONTH IN WHICH THE HOSTING SERVICES ARE PROVIDED, except that, with respect to Additional Fees (as defined in Exhibit A), eGain will invoice Customer for such Fees in the month after the month in which such fees accrue as provided in Exhibit A.
perturbation.changed_text: Customer agrees to pay the fees and other charges for the Hosting Services and other services provided under this Agreement as specified in Exhibit A of this Agreement. CUSTOMER AGREES TO PAY FOR HOSTING SERVICES WITHIN 30 DAYS OF RECEIPT OF AN INVOICE FROM EGAin, except that, with respect to Additional Fees (as defined in Exhibit A), eGain will invoice Customer for such Fees in the month after the month in which such fees accrue as provided in Exhibit A.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity in the payment terms. Initially, the contract specifies payment 'on or before the first day of the month.' The modification states payment is due 'within 30 days of receipt of an invoice'. This creates an in-text contradiction because a payment made on the first day of the month where the services are provided directly contradicts paying upon receipt of an invoice. This contradiction is significant because a payment can be made before or after the hosting services are provided. It can have financial implications, affecting when the company receives revenue and manages cash flow. In a case where services are provided throughout the month, it is not clear which term will apply. In the Exhibit A, the contradiction remains, so the user is unable to properly enforce the payment rules.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pricing and Payment. Customer agrees to pay the fees and other charges for the Hosting Services and other services provided under this Agreement as specified in Exhibit A of this Agreement. CUSTOMER AGREES TO PAY FOR HOSTING SERVICES WITHIN 30 DAYS OF RECEIPT OF AN INVOICE FROM EGAin, except that, with respect to Additional Fees (as defined in Exhibit A), eGain will invoice Customer for such Fees in the month after the month in which such fees accrue as provided in Exhibit A. All amounts payable hereunder are exclusive of any and all taxes, and Customer is responsible for payment of such taxes (excluding taxes based on eGain's net income). All prices are stated, and Customer shall pay, in United States dollars. Payment received by eGain after the due date shall be subject to a late fee equal to one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month, or, if less, the maximum amount allowed by applicable law. At the end of the initial one-year term of this Agreement and any subsequent one-year terms, eGain may adjust the monthly fee payable under this Agreement by providing Customer written notice of such adjustment at least sixty (60) days prior to the beginning of the new term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct in-text contradiction. The original text specifies payment 'on or before the first day of the month'. The modified text states payment is due 'within 30 days of receipt of an invoice'. This creates a conflict because paying on the first day of the month where services are provided directly contradicts paying within 30 days of receiving an invoice, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes over payment timing. This adheres to the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In addition, either party may terminate this Agreement by giving to the other party written notice eGAIN COMMUNICATIONS CORPORATION HOSTING AGREEMENT of such termination upon the other party's material breach of any material term (subject to the other party's right to cure within thirty (30) days after receipt of such notice), the other party's insolvency, or the institution of any bankruptcy or similar proceedings by or against the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition, either party may terminate this Agreement by giving to the other party written notice of such termination upon the other party's material breach of any material term (subject to the other party's right to cure within sixty (60) days after receipt of such notice), the other party's insolvency, or the institution of any bankruptcy or similar proceedings by or against the other party. However, eGain may terminate this agreement with ten (10) days notice without any of the above reasons.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an inconsistency in the termination clause. The original clause states that either party can terminate the agreement for a material breach, insolvency, or bankruptcy, providing a thirty (30) day cure period. By adding a sentence that 'eGain may terminate this agreement with ten (10) days notice without any of the above reasons', this creates an in-text contradiction and gives eGain an additional, unilateral right to terminate without cause and with a shorter notice period. This affects how a normal termination procedure would be handled, given that eGain can terminate the agreement without providing any reason. Also, the contradiction undermines the other party's right to cure period. In section 10.2, it also contradics about the 'effect of termination', where the obligations of both parties after a normal termination are set.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and Termination. This Agreement shall continue in effect from the Effective Date for a one (1) year period, unless earlier terminated as set forth below, and thereafter shall renew automatically for successive one (1) year periods unless either party gives the other party at least thirty (30) days prior written notice of its intent not to renew the Agreement. In addition, either party may terminate this Agreement by giving to the other party written notice of such termination upon the other party's material breach of any material term (subject to the other party's right to cure within sixty (60) days after receipt of such notice), the other party's insolvency, or the institution of any bankruptcy or similar proceedings by or against the other party. However, eGain may terminate this agreement with ten (10) days notice without any of the above reasons.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text implies a consistent termination process for both parties, with a 30-day cure period for breaches. The added clause allows eGain to terminate the agreement with only ten days' notice and without any specific cause, directly conflicting with the originally agreed-upon termination procedure and undermining the other party's right to cure. The contradicted text, which includes the original termination clause as well as a clause about renewal, is now directly contradicted by the new clause giving eGain unilateral termination rights. The contradiction makes the agreement ambiguous about which termination clause applies and when, and it falls under the definition of inconsistencies.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California exclusive of its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, unless the dispute involves intellectual property rights, in which case California law shall apply exclusive of its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction regarding the governing law. The original text states that the agreement is governed by the laws of California. The modification states that New York law applies, unless the dispute involves intellectual property, in which case California law governs. This is an in-text contradiction since it sets conflicting rules of which jurisdiction will govern the agreement based on the dispute in question. This conflict can affect how the contract is interpreted and enforced, because both parties won't be sure which law to adhere to. Also the introduction of different laws being applied can affect Section 7's Limitation of Liability clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous. This Agreement, including Exhibit A and any other exhibits hereto, constitutes the entire agreement of the parties, and supersedes any prior or contemporaneous agreements between the parties, with respect to the subject of this Agreement. Except as otherwise expressly provided herein, this Agreement may be modified only by a writing signed by an authorized representative of each party. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, unless the dispute involves intellectual property rights, in which case California law shall apply exclusive of its conflict of laws principles. Notices under this Agreement shall be in writing, addressed to the party at its last-provided address, and shall be deemed given when delivered personally, or by e-mail (with confirmation of receipt) or conventional mail (registered or certified, postage prepaid with return receipt requested). Nothing contained in this Agreement is intended or is to be construed to constitute eGain and Customer as partners or joint venturers or either party as an agent of the other. If any provision of this Agreement shall be declared invalid, illegal or unenforceable, all remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect. All waivers of any rights or breach hereunder must be in writing to be effective, and no failure to enforce any right or provision shall be deemed to be a waiver of the same or other right or provision on that or any other occasion. Neither party may assign or otherwise transfer its rights and/or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no consent shall be required for an assignment of this Agreement made pursuant to a merger, consolidation, or the acquisition of all or substantially all of the business and assets of a party. This Agreement will bind and inure to the benefit of the parties and their successors and permitted assigns. Each party agrees to the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement. Customer: Eliance Corporation Name:/s/ Title: ___________________________ _________ Signature:______________________ Date:__________ eGain Communications Corporation: Name:/s/ Title: ___________________________ _________ Signature:______________________ Date:__________
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction in the governing law provision. The original text specifies California law, while the changed text specifies New York law, unless the dispute involves intellectual property, in which case California law applies. The contradicted text, which now includes the changed text, therefore contains conflicting rules regarding the governing law, which affects how the contract is interpreted and enforced, and creates an ambiguity regarding the application of Section 7's Limitation of Liability clause. This ambiguity is a direct conflict within the document itself.
